title: What to know about Angelica sinensis before using it medicinally
intro_desc: Angelica sinensis, commonly known as Chinese angelica, is a medicinal herb used to treat various health conditions, including menstrual disorders, fever, and respiratory infections. Its main medicinal uses include relieving menstrual cramps, reducing fever, and soothing respiratory issues. The herb offers several health benefits, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and immunomodulatory effects. The main therapeutic properties of Angelica sinensis include its analgesic, anti-pyretic, and expectorant properties. The herb contains bioactive compounds such as ferulic acid, sinensetin, and ligustilide, and is often prepared as a tea or dried root for consumption.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Journal of ethnopharmacology, the rhizomes of Zingiber officinale have been found to provide several health benefits, including reducing inflammation and fever, inhibiting the growth of both Gram-positive and Gram-negative bacteria, and lowering blood glucose levels. Additionally, the extract has been shown to exhibit analgesic properties by reducing carrageenan-induced paw swelling in rats. Furthermore, its antimicrobial properties have been observed to be dose-dependent, affecting prostaglandin release and inhibiting microbial growth.
uses_description: The main medicinal uses of Angelica sinensis are treating anemia due to its rich iron content and antioxidant properties, which help increase red blood cell count and reduce oxidative stress. Additionally, it is used to alleviate fatigue and digestive issues, such as constipation and bloating, thanks to its prebiotic and anti-inflammatory properties, which promote gut health and alleviate symptoms of irritable bowel syndrome. Angelica sinensis is also employed to treat menstrual disorders, including heavy menstrual bleeding and irregular periods, as it has been shown to decrease uterine contractions and improve estrogen levels. Furthermore, its anxiolytic and bronchodilatory properties make it a useful treatment for anxiety and asthma, reducing symptoms of anxiety and improving lung function. By modulating the body's hormonal response, Angelica sinensis has been found to relieve menstrual cramps, hypertension, diarrhea, and menopause symptoms, including hot flashes and night sweats.

uses_list.description: It is used to treat anemia due to its iron-rich properties, which help to increase hemoglobin levels and improve red blood cell production.
Its ability to improve blood circulation and increase oxygen delivery to tissues makes it effective in reducing fatigue and improving overall energy levels.
The plant's anti-inflammatory properties and saponins help to soothe digestive issues, such as irritable bowel syndrome, by reducing inflammation and improving gut health.
Its ability to regulate menstrual cycles and reduce symptoms of premenstrual syndrome, such as bloating and breast tenderness, makes it effective in treating menstrual disorders.
Its flavonoids and coumarins have a calming effect on the nervous system, which helps to reduce anxiety and promote relaxation.
The plant's anti-inflammatory properties and saponins help to reduce inflammation and improve lung function, making it effective in treating asthma.
Its ability to relax smooth muscle and reduce inflammation makes it effective in treating menstrual cramps and other symptoms of dysmenorrhea.
Its flavonoids and coumarins help to dilate blood vessels and reduce blood pressure, making it effective in treating hypertension.
The plant's saponins and tannins help to soothe and protect the gastrointestinal tract, reducing inflammation and improving gut health, which makes it effective in treating diarrhea.
Its ability to regulate hormonal changes and reduce symptoms of menopause, such as hot flashes and night sweats, makes it effective in treating menopause symptoms.

uses_modern_western_medicine_description: In modern western medicine, Angelica sinensis is used to treat anemia, which is often alleviated by increasing red blood cell count and hemoglobin levels. Menopausal symptoms, such as hot flashes and night sweats, are also effectively managed with Angelica sinensis, showing its estrogen-like properties. Research suggests that it may also help alleviate cardiovascular disease by reducing inflammation and improving lipid profiles, although more studies are needed to confirm this. Additionally, Angelica sinensis has been shown to reduce menopause-related symptoms, including vasomotor and musculoskeletal issues, while also lowering hypertension by inhibiting angiotensin-converting enzyme. Furthermore, it has been used to provide menstrual relief, improve digestive issues through its prebiotic properties, and even exhibit anti-cancer and anti-diabetic effects, although its efficacy in these areas requires further investigation, and it has also been found to have anxiolytic properties.
uses_traditional_chinese_medicine_descri: In traditional Chinese medicine, Angelica sinensis is used to treat anemia, a condition characterized by a deficiency of red blood cells. The plant's properties also make it effective in alleviating fatigue, often caused by a lack of oxygen delivery to the body's tissues. Furthermore, Angelica sinensis is employed to address digestive issues, such as bloating and cramps, which can be associated with its ability to regulate the body's internal environment. Its antispasmodic and expectorant properties also render it useful in treating asthma and cough, while its uterotonic effects can help to alleviate menstrual disorders and menstrual cramps. Additionally, the plant is used to treat rheumatism and respiratory problems, as well as diarrhea, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
uses_ayurvedic_medicine_description: In ayurvedic medicine, Angelica sinensis is used to treat anemia, with its high iron content and antioxidant properties helping to increase red blood cell production. It is also used to alleviate digestive issues such as constipation and bloating, as well as to combat fatigue and enhance energy levels. Additionally, Angelica sinensis is utilized to address menstrual issues, including irregular periods and heavy bleeding, due to its emmenagogic and estrogenic properties. Furthermore, it is used to help manage hypertension by reducing blood pressure and alleviating symptoms of rheumatism, such as joint pain and inflammation. Its anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties also make it effective in treating fever, diarrhea, and asthma, as well as menstrual disorders like dysmenorrhea and amenorrhea.
uses_unani_medicine_description: In unani medicine, Angelica sinensis is used to treat anemia, particularly iron-deficiency anemia, by improving iron absorption and utilization. It is also employed to alleviate menstrual disorders, such as dysmenorrhea, by regulating the menstrual cycle and reducing cramping. Furthermore, Angelica sinensis is used to combat fatigue, digestive issues, and diarrhea, as it possesses anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that help to soothe the gastrointestinal tract. Additionally, it is effective in managing asthma, menorrhagia, cough, and epilepsy by modulating the body's response to stress and regulating the autonomic nervous system. Its anxiolytic properties also make it a valuable treatment for anxiety.
uses_homeopathic_medicine_description: In homeopathic medicine, Angelica sinensis is used to treat fatigue, which is often alleviated due to its adaptogenic properties that help regulate the body's response to stress. The plant's ability to calm the nervous system also makes it effective in managing anxiety, and its anti-inflammatory properties provide relief from menstrual cramps. Additionally, Angelica sinensis is used to address digestive issues, such as bloating and indigestion, by stimulating digestion and reducing inflammation. Furthermore, it is used to treat anemia by increasing red blood cell count and improving oxygen delivery to tissues, and it is also used to provide menstrual relief by regulating hormonal imbalances. The plant's anti-inflammatory and vasodilatory effects also make it beneficial in treating menopause, rheumatism, hypertension, and cough, by reducing inflammation and improving blood flow to affected areas.
benefits_description: Angelica sinensis reduces inflammation by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines, thereby alleviating conditions such as arthritis. Improving blood flow is another benefit, as it increases the delivery of oxygen and nutrients to tissues, promoting healthy cell growth and repair. Additionally, Angelica sinensis lowers blood pressure by blocking the action of angiotensin II, a potent vasoconstrictor. Enhancing immune function is facilitated by the plant's ability to stimulate the production of interferon-gamma, a key cytokine in the immune response, thereby protecting against infections. Angelica sinensis also relieves menstrual pain by inhibiting the release of pain-causing substances and supports liver health by inducing the expression of detoxification enzymes, which helps to prevent cell damage and decrease oxidative stress, while also improving cognitive function by increasing the expression of neuroprotective genes that promote neuronal health, and finally, promotes wound healing by stimulating the production of growth factors that facilitate tissue repair.

benefits_list.description: This plant contains bioactive compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce inflammation and alleviate conditions such as arthritis and other inflammatory diseases. The anti-inflammatory compounds in this plant, including ferulic acid and apiol, can inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines, thus reducing inflammation and promoting healing.

The bioactive compounds in this plant, such as ferulic acid and apiol, have vasodilatory properties, which can help to improve blood flow and reduce blood pressure. Improved blood flow can increase oxygenation and nutrient delivery to tissues, promoting healing and reducing the risk of cardiovascular disease.

The vasodilatory compounds in this plant can help to relax blood vessels and reduce blood pressure. Regular consumption of this plant may help to lower blood pressure in individuals with hypertension, thereby reducing the risk of cardiovascular disease and stroke.

This plant contains bioactive compounds that have immunomodulatory properties, which can help to enhance immune function and reduce the risk of infections. The immunomodulatory compounds in this plant, including ferulic acid and apiol, can stimulate the production of white blood cells and activate immune cells, thereby enhancing immune function and reducing the risk of infections.

The bioactive compounds in this plant, including ferulic acid and apiol, have analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to relieve menstrual pain and other symptoms associated with premenstrual syndrome (PMS). The analgesic and anti-inflammatory compounds in this plant can inhibit the production of pain-causing chemicals and reduce inflammation, thereby relieving menstrual pain and promoting comfort.

The bioactive compounds in this plant, including ferulic acid and apiol, have hepatoprotective properties, which can help to support liver health and reduce the risk of liver disease. The hepatoprotective compounds in this plant can inhibit the production of liver-damaging chemicals and reduce liver inflammation, thereby supporting liver health and reducing the risk of liver disease.

The bioactive compounds in this plant, including ferulic acid and apiol, have antioxidant properties, which can help to prevent cell damage and reduce the risk of oxidative stress-related diseases. The antioxidant compounds in this plant can neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress, thereby preventing cell damage and promoting overall health.

The antioxidant compounds in this plant, including ferulic acid and apiol, can help to reduce oxidative stress and promote overall health. Oxidative stress can cause cell damage and increase the risk of diseases such as cancer and neurodegenerative disorders. The antioxidant compounds in this plant can neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress, thereby promoting overall health and reducing the risk of disease.

The bioactive compounds in this plant, including ferulic acid and apiol, have neuroprotective properties, which can help to improve cognitive function and reduce the risk of neurodegenerative disorders. The neuroprotective compounds in this plant can inhibit the production of neurotoxic chemicals and reduce neuroinflammation, thereby improving cognitive function and reducing the risk of neurodegenerative disorders.

The bioactive compounds in this plant, including ferulic acid and apiol, have wound-healing properties, which can help to promote wound healing and reduce the risk of infection. The wound-healing compounds in this plant can stimulate the production of growth factors and promote tissue repair, thereby promoting wound healing and reducing the risk of infection.

benefits_circulatory_system_description: Angelica sinensis lower blood pressure by exerting antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects on the cardiovascular system. It improves circulation by stimulating the production of nitric oxide, a potent vasodilator that widens blood vessels and reduces vascular resistance. The plant prevents blood clots through its anti-coagulant properties, which inhibit the activity of thrombin and prevent the formation of fibrin. By reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, Angelica sinensis promotes wound healing and enhances endothelial function, allowing blood vessels to relax and dilate more efficiently. The plant's anti-platelet aggregation properties and ability to improve microcirculation also contribute to its cardiovascular benefits, including reducing oxidative stress and enhancing overall cardiovascular function by reducing platelet aggregation.
benefits_digestive_system_description: Angelica sinensis regulates digestion by containing iridoid glycosides with anti-inflammatory properties that soothe inflammation in the gut, improving absorption of nutrients. Its antioxidant and antimicrobial properties protect the gut from oxidative stress and bacterial infections, while also reducing bloating caused by gas accumulation. Additionally, Angelica sinensis enhances motility by stimulating the movement of digestive enzymes and muscles, relaxing muscles that can cause spasms and discomfort. The plant's anti-inflammatory compounds also protect the gut lining from damage, supporting the growth of beneficial probiotics that boost energy levels. Furthermore, Angelica sinensis's immunomodulatory properties fight infections by regulating the immune response and promoting the production of antibodies that help to eliminate pathogens.
benefits_endocrine_system_description: Angelica sinensis regulates hormones through its phytoestrogenic properties, which mimic the effects of estrogen in the body, thus alleviating menopause symptoms by reducing hot flashes and night sweats. This plant balances thyroid function by modulating the expression of thyroid-related genes and inhibiting the activity of thyroid peroxidase, an enzyme involved in thyroid hormone production. The antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties of Angelica sinensis also stabilize metabolism by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the body, which can contribute to metabolic disorders. Additionally, the plant's adaptogenic properties support adrenal function by reducing cortisol levels and improving the body's response to stress, making it beneficial for individuals with adrenal fatigue. Furthermore, Angelica sinensis treats insulin resistance and manages diabetes by enhancing insulin sensitivity and glucose uptake in skeletal muscle, thus normalizing pituitary function through its regulatory effects on the hypothalamic-pituitary-adrenal axis.
benefits_integumentary_system_descriptio: Angelica sinensis reduces wrinkles due to its high content of antioxidants and flavonoids, which help to neutralize free radicals and protect the skin from oxidative damage. The plant's anti-inflammatory properties also contribute to the healing of burns by reducing inflammation and promoting tissue repair. Angelica sinensis repairs skin by stimulating collagen production, improving skin elasticity, and enhancing wound closure. The plant's ability to prevent scars is attributed to its antimicrobial and antioxidant properties, which help to control infection and promote tissue regeneration. The combination of its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties also enables Angelica sinensis to treat eczema, soothe irritation, fade bruises, cure acne, and protect skin.
benefits_lymphatic_system_description: Angelica sinensis enhances lymph flow by stimulating lymphatic vessels to improve the removal of waste products and toxins, which also boosts immune defense by increasing white blood cell count through its immunomodulatory properties. Its anti-inflammatory properties reduce inflammation levels, alleviating swelling and pain, while its antioxidant properties activate detoxification process, neutralizing free radicals that damage cells. Additionally, Angelica sinensis improves circulation efficiency, allowing for the efficient transport of oxygen and nutrients to tissues, and stimulates lymph node function, facilitating the removal of pathogens and toxins. Furthermore, it regulates fluid balance by promoting the efficient removal of excess fluids, reducing edema, and supports tissue repair by providing essential nutrients and growth factors. Through its antimicrobial and antioxidant properties, Angelica sinensis fights free radical damage, protecting cells from oxidative stress.
benefits_musculoskeletal_system_descript: Angelica sinensis relieve arthritis by inhibiting pro-inflammatory cytokines and prostaglandins, which contribute to the pain and inflammation of arthritic joints. The anti-inflammatory properties of Angelica sinensis reduce inflammation by suppressing the activity of enzymes that break down joint tissues, thereby alleviating pain and discomfort. Angelica sinensis ease muscle spasms by relaxing muscle tissue and improving blood flow, which also reduces muscle fatigue and soreness. In addition to these benefits, Angelica sinensis improve joint mobility and function by promoting the production of glycosaminoglycans, which maintain joint health and prevent degenerative changes. Furthermore, Angelica sinensis strengthen bones by enhancing osteoblast activity and suppressing osteoclast activity, which also enhance flexibility and prevent osteoporosis, and its ability to heal fractures by stimulating collagen synthesis and promoting tissue repair.
benefits_nervous_system_description: Angelica sinensis protects nerve cells by exhibiting antioxidant properties, which counteract oxidative stress and prevent cell damage. It improves neuroplasticity by enhancing the expression of neurotrophic factors, facilitating the growth and differentiation of neurons. Enhancing cognitive function is attributed to Angelica sinensis's ability to modulate neurotransmitter levels, particularly serotonin and dopamine, which play crucial roles in attention and memory. Reducing neuroinflammation is a result of its anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory properties, which inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and promote an anti-inflammatory environment. Angelica sinensis supports neuromuscular health, promotes neuroregeneration, decreases neurodegeneration, relaxes the nervous system, and improves sleep quality through its adaptogenic and anti-apoptotic properties, which help maintain homeostasis and prevent cell death.
benefits_reproductive_system_description: Angelica sinensis regulate menstrual cycles by exerting anti-inflammatory effects on the uterine lining, which helps to balance hormonal fluctuations. This plant improve fertility in women by enhancing ovulation and stimulating progesterone production, thus supporting reproductive health. Additionally, Angelica sinensis reduce menopause symptoms by acting as an antioxidant and mitigating oxidative stress, which contributes to hormonal balance. The plant enhance ovulation by inhibiting the production of luteinizing hormone-releasing hormone, thereby promoting a more favorable hormonal environment. Furthermore, Angelica sinensis increase libido by stimulating the release of neurotransmitters, such as dopamine and serotonin, which regulate sexual desire and function, while also relieve dysmenorrhea by blocking pain pathways and reducing inflammation, ultimately promote hormonal balance and boost testosterone levels in both men and women.
benefits_respiratory_system_description: Angelica sinensis relieves congestion by its expectorant properties, which help to expel mucus from the lungs and airways. Its anti-inflammatory properties reduce inflammation, making it an effective treatment for respiratory conditions. Furthermore, Angelica sinensis prevents asthma by inhibiting the release of histamine and other chemical mediators that contribute to the development of asthma symptoms. Additionally, its antimicrobial properties fight infections, such as those caused by bacteria and viruses, that can exacerbate respiratory conditions. Angelica sinensis also eases breathing by relaxing the airway smooth muscles and suppressing coughs, while also curing bronchitis by destroying the bacteria that cause the infection, treating allergies by modulating the immune response, protecting lungs by reducing oxidative stress, and healing tuberculosis by its antioxidant properties.
benefits_urinary_system_description: Angelica sinensis prevents kidney damage due to its antioxidant properties, which neutralize free radicals that cause oxidative stress and impair kidney function. It reduces urine frequency by relaxing bladder muscles, alleviating overactive bladder symptoms, and enhancing urine flow. The plant supports kidney function by stimulating urine production, which helps to remove toxins and waste products from the body, thereby protecting kidney cells from damage. Angelica sinensis also alleviates UTI symptoms by inhibiting the growth of pathogenic bacteria, such as E. coli, and reduces bladder inflammation by exerting anti-inflammatory properties, thus increasing bladder capacity and preventing kidney damage. Furthermore, it protects kidney cells from oxidative stress and promotes the regeneration of damaged kidney tissues, which increases bladder capacity and enhances urine flow.
properties_desc: The medicinal properties of Angelica sinensis are attributed to its antimicrobial activity, which has been linked to the presence of volatile oils and saponins that inhibit the growth of pathogens and bacteria. The antioxidant properties of this plant are due to the flavonoids and phenolic acids that scavenge free radicals and protect cells from oxidative damage, thus contributing to its anti-inflammatory effects, which are mediated by the inhibition of pro-inflammatory enzymes and cytokines. Additionally, Angelica sinensis exhibits analgesic and antispasmodic properties, which are thought to be related to the presence of sesquiterpenes and alkaloids that modulate pain and smooth muscle contraction. The antipyretic effects of this plant are also noteworthy, and are attributed to the presence of sesquiterpenes that reduce fever by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines. Furthermore, Angelica sinensis has been shown to possess anticancer, immunomodulatory, cardioprotective, and neuroprotective properties, which are thought to be related to the presence of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other bioactive compounds that inhibit cancer cell growth, modulate the immune response, protect the cardiovascular system, and prevent neurodegenerative diseases.

properties_list.description: Angelica sinensis has been shown to exhibit antimicrobial activity, including against bacteria such as Staphylococcus aureus and Escherichia coli, as well as against fungi like Candida albicans, due to the presence of compounds like ferulic acid and ligustilide.

The antioxidant properties of Angelica sinensis are attributed to its high content of polyphenols, flavonoids, and saponins, which can scavenge free radicals and protect against oxidative stress and cell damage.

Angelica sinensis has been found to possess anti-inflammatory properties, attributed to the presence of compounds like ferulic acid, ligustilide, and senkyunolide A, which can inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and enzymes.

Angelica sinensis has been traditionally used to treat pain and has been found to exhibit analgesic properties, which are attributed to the presence of compounds like ferulic acid and ligustilide, which can inhibit the production of pain mediators.
The antispasmodic properties of Angelica sinensis are attributed to the presence of compounds like ferulic acid and ligustilide, which can relax smooth muscle and prevent spasms.

Angelica sinensis has been found to exhibit antipyretic properties, attributed to the presence of compounds like ferulic acid and ligustilide, which can reduce fever by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines.

Angelica sinensis has been found to exhibit anticancer properties, attributed to the presence of compounds like ferulic acid and senkyunolide A, which can inhibit the growth of cancer cells and induce apoptosis.

Angelica sinensis has been found to exhibit immunomodulatory properties, attributed to the presence of compounds like ferulic acid and ligustilide, which can modulate the immune response and enhance the production of antibodies.

The cardioprotective properties of Angelica sinensis are attributed to the presence of compounds like ferulic acid and ligustilide, which can protect against cardiovascular disease by reducing blood pressure, inhibiting platelet aggregation, and preventing lipid peroxidation.

Angelica sinensis has been found to exhibit neuroprotective properties, attributed to the presence of compounds like ferulic acid and ligustilide, which can protect against neurodegenerative diseases by reducing oxidative stress, inhibiting inflammation, and promoting neuronal survival.

constituents.description: Coumarins are a class of organic compounds with anticoagulant, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties
Saponins in Angelica sinensis have hemolytic, foaming, and surface-active properties.
Phenolic acids are antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds, possessing antimicrobial, antiviral, and anti-cancer properties.
Flavonoids in Angelica sinensis are reported to possess anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory properties.
Lignans are a type of polyphenolic compound with antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and estrogenic properties
Ferulic acid is a phenolic compound with antioxidant, anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
Ligustilide is a sesquiterpene lactone with sedative, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties.
Polysaccharides from Angelica sinensis have immunomodulatory, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties.
Butylphthalide is a sesquiterpene lactone with sedative, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties.
Volatile oils in Angelica sinensis have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.

constituents_desc: The main medicinal constituents of Angelica sinensis are coumarins, saponins, phenolic acids, flavonoids, and lignans. These constituents are primarily responsible for its medicinal properties due to their ability to exhibit anti-inflammatory, anti-oxidative, and anti-viral effects. Coumarins, such as angelol and ligustilide, possess anti-inflammatory and neuroprotective properties, while saponins, including acylsaponins and diosgenin, exhibit anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects. Phenolic acids, flavonoids, and lignans, including ferulic acid, quercetin, and sesamin, also contribute to the plant's anti-oxidative and anti-inflammatory activities. The combination of these constituents in Angelica sinensis has been shown to exhibit synergistic effects, enhancing its overall medicinal efficacy.
parts_desc: The most used parts of Angelica sinensis for medicinal purposes are roots, rhizomes, and leaves. The roots primarily contain saponins, volatile oils, and ferulic acid, which exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and analgesic properties. The rhizomes are rich in polysaccharides, ferulic acid, and volatile oils, which display anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory effects. The leaves contain flavonoids, sesquiterpenes, and volatile oils, which exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. These bioactive compounds in the different parts of Angelica sinensis contribute to its use in traditional medicine for treating various conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues.

parts_list.description: The roots of this plant contain major bioactive compounds such as ferulic acid, ligustilide, and butylphthalide, which exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and neuroprotective properties, contributing to its main medicinal properties of anti-anxiety, anti-fatigue, and anti-aging effects, ultimately leading to primary health benefits including improved sleep quality, enhanced cognitive function, and reduced risk of cardiovascular diseases.

The rhizomes of this plant are rich in major bioactive compounds like ligustilide, butylphthalide, and ferulic acid, which display anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and anti-cancer properties, characterizing its main medicinal properties of anti-tumor, anti-oxidative stress, and anti-inflammatory effects, resulting in primary health benefits including reduced risk of cancer, improved immune function, and alleviated symptoms of menopausal disorders.

The leaves of this plant contain major bioactive compounds including ligustilide, butylphthalide, and ferulic acid, which exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and anti-bacterial properties, defining its main medicinal properties of anti-inflammatory, anti-microbial, and anti-allergic effects, ultimately leading to primary health benefits including reduced risk of infections, alleviated symptoms of allergies, and improved skin health.

preparations.description: Tincture made from this plant is used to treat menstrual disorders, rheumatism and to improve cardiovascular function.
preparations.overview: Angelica sinensis tincture is a traditional herbal remedy used to treat fatigue, inflammation, digestive issues, hypertension, anemia, anxiety, menstrual disorders, menstrual cramps, cough, and insomnia. It's one of the most commonly used herbal tinctures globally. This widespread use is due to its proven efficacy in alleviating various health conditions. A common effect of using Angelica sinensis tincture is its ability to promote relaxation and reduce stress. The preparation is typically made from the roots and rhizomes of the Angelica sinensis plant. Although the preparation process involves moderately complex steps, including soaking, drying, and solvent extraction, the end result is a concentrated extract that can be easily administered as a liquid supplement.
recipe: Gather 1 part of clean, dried Angelica sinensis roots and rhizomes, and 2 parts of a neutral solvent such as vodka or glycerin.
Combine the Angelica sinensis roots and rhizomes with the solvent in a clean glass jar, and seal it tightly.
Steep the mixture in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking the jar daily to facilitate extraction.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into another clean glass container, discarding the solids.
Store the Angelica sinensis tincture in a cool, dark place and take 20-30 drops, 2-3 times a day, as needed.

preparations.description: Capsule made from this plant is used to treat menstrual disorders, reduce inflammation, and alleviate symptoms of cardiovascular disease and diabetes.
preparations.overview: Angelica sinensis capsule is used to treat fatigue, anemia, menstrual disorder, hypertension, digestive issues, asthma, diabetes, inflammation, cardiovascular disease and menstrual cramps. This preparation is widely used as one of the most common herbal remedies in traditional Chinese medicine. Its effects include reducing blood pressure, improving digestion, alleviating menstrual cramps and inflammation. Angelica sinensis capsule is made from the roots and rhizomes of Angelica sinensis, a plant native to China. The process of making this capsule is moderately complex, requiring careful selection and processing of the plant materials to ensure optimal potency and efficacy.
recipe: Dry and clean 30 grams of Angelica sinensis roots and rhizomes in a well-ventilated area.
Grind the dried roots and rhizomes into fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix the powder with 10 grams of starch or other excipients to facilitate capsule filling.
Fill gelatin or vegetable-based capsules with the powder mixture, making sure to follow a consistent dosage of 1-2 capsules, 2-3 times a day.
Seal the capsules and store them in a cool, dry place to preserve their potency and shelf life.

preparations.description: Decoction made from this plant is traditionally used to treat menstrual disorders, alleviate rheumatic pains, and lower blood pressure.
preparations.overview: Angelica sinensis decoction is a traditional herbal preparation used to treat various health conditions including anemia, fatigue, hypertension, menstrual disorders, asthma, digestive issues, diarrhea, rheumatism, inflammation, and menopause. It is a commonly used preparation in traditional Chinese medicine. The decoction has a common effect of promoting blood circulation and reducing blood pressure. This preparation is typically made with the roots and rhizomes of Angelica sinensis. The process of making the decoction is moderately difficult, requiring precise measurement and combination of ingredients to achieve the desired therapeutic effect.
recipe: Gather 6-10 grams of Angelica sinensis roots and 2-3 grams of rhizomes.
Rinse the roots and rhizomes under running water, then pat them dry with a paper towel.
Combine the roots and rhizomes in a pot and add 1 liter of water.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat and simmer for 30 minutes.
Strain the decoction and discard the solids, then drink 1/2 to 1 cup, 2-3 times a day, as needed.

preparations.description: Tea made from this plant is used to treat menstrual disorders, reduce fever, alleviate rheumatic pain, and improve blood circulation.
preparations.overview: Angelica sinensis tea is a herbal remedy commonly used to treat various health issues. It's employed to alleviate fatigue, menstrual cramps, digestive issues, anemia, anxiety, hypertension, high blood pressure, inflammation, rheumatism, and sleep disorders. This preparation is widely used due to its proven efficacy and is often found in traditional Chinese medicine. One of its most common effects is the relaxation of the body and mind, which can be attributed to its sedative properties. Angelica sinensis tea is made from the roots, rhizomes, and leaves of the Angelica sinensis plant. Its preparation is relatively simple, typically involving boiling the plant material in water to create a tea that can be consumed hot or cold.
recipe: Gather 5-10 grams of dried Angelica sinensis roots, rhizomes, and leaves, depending on personal preference and dosage.
Rinse the herbs with lukewarm water to remove any impurities, and then dry them thoroughly.
Combine the dried herbs in a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup, and add 1 liter of boiling water.
Steep the herbs for 5-10 minutes, or until the desired strength and flavor are achieved.
Strain the tea and serve immediately, or store it in the refrigerator for later use.

preparation_desc: The most used herbal preparation of Angelica sinensis for medicinal purposes are tinctures, which are concentrated extracts typically used to treat menstrual disorders and reduce inflammation. Capsules, containing dried Angelica sinensis powder, are often employed to alleviate symptoms of arthritis and menopausal hot flashes. Decoctions, a method of extracting active compounds through prolonged boiling, are utilized to treat various conditions, including fever and digestive issues. Additionally, Angelica sinensis can be prepared as a tea, an aqueous infusion used to soothe the stomach and promote relaxation. These medicinal preparations are commonly used due to the plant's purported adaptogenic and anti-inflammatory properties.

side_effects.side_effect_description: Excessive vomiting occurs in high doses of Angelica sinensis.
Causes immune system to react to Angelica sinensis components, resulting in an allergic response.
Hemorrhagic complications due to Angelica sinensis consumption have been reported in patients taking anticoagulant medications.
Angelica sinensis contains angelic acid and its derivatives which induce liver damage and necrosis.
Angelica sinensis contains compounds that are toxic to kidney cells, potentially causing kidney damage or failure.
Angelica sinensis contains furanocoumarins that cause phototoxic reactions when skin is exposed to sunlight.

side_effects_overview: The possible side effects of improperly using Angelica sinensis are vomiting, particularly if ingested in large quantities, which can lead to stomach discomfort and nausea. Improper use of this plant may also cause abortions in pregnant women due to its emmenagogic properties. Allergic reactions, such as skin irritation or respiratory issues, can occur in individuals sensitized to its active compounds. Additionally, hypotension may be induced in people with pre-existing low blood pressure, exacerbating their condition. Furthermore, improperly used Angelica sinensis can cause hemorrhaging, particularly in individuals with bleeding disorders or those taking anticoagulant medications, leading to excessive bleeding or bruising; this plant has also been found to be hepatotoxic, damaging liver cells and potentially causing liver failure; it can also be nephrotoxic, impairing kidney function and potentially leading to kidney disease; and, in some cases, it may even cause phototoxicity, leading to skin irritation or burns upon exposure to sunlight.

precautions.precaution_description: People with kidney disease, liver disease, or bleeding disorders should consult a doctor before using Angelica sinensis.
Pregnant women should be advised to avoid Angelica sinensis due to potential effects on uterus contractions and labor.
Monitor liver enzymes in patients with pre-existing liver disease or those taking hepatotoxic medications due to potential hepatotoxic effects of Angelica sinensis.
Taking more than the recommended dose can cause gastrointestinal symptoms, renal failure, and bleeding.
Individuals with kidney disease or kidney impairment should monitor kidney function due to potential nephrotoxicity of Angelica sinensis.
Concomitant use with anticoagulant medications, such as warfarin, increases the risk of bleeding.
Dose Angelica sinensis according to evidence-based guidelines, typically 3-9 grams of dried root per day.

precautions_overview: The precautions to take before using Angelica sinensis medicinally are consulting a doctor to discuss potential benefits and risks, particularly for individuals with bleeding disorders or taking anticoagulant medications. Determining pregnancy status is essential as high doses may stimulate uterine contractions. Assessing liver function is crucial for patients with pre-existing liver conditions or those taking medications that may interact with Angelica sinensis. To avoid overdosing, it's recommended to start with low doses and gradually increase as needed, as excessive consumption may exacerbate bleeding tendencies. Monitoring kidney function and understanding dosing are also vital considerations, as high doses may accumulate in the body and cause kidney damage.
identification: Angelica sinensis is a perennial plant belonging to the Apiaceae family, characterized by its large, coarse, and hairy leaves, which are pinnate with 5-7 pairs of leaflets and a long petiole. It typically grows to a height of 1.5-2.5 meters, with a stout stem and a large, hollow, and grooved root. This plant is native to the mountainous regions of China, specifically in the Sichuan, Guizhou, and Yunnan provinces, where it thrives in humid, subtropical climates at elevations between 1,500 and 3,000 meters above sea level. The plant's inflorescence is a large, compound umbel, composed of numerous small, yellowish-green flowers. It is often found in association with other plants, such as Astragalus and Gentiana, in the mountainous forests and meadows of its native range.
cultivation: Angelica sinensis is a perennial plant native to China, which typically thrives in growth habits that include a tall stem (up to 2 meters) and a clump-forming habit. This plant requires well-drained soil with a pH range of 6.0-7.0 and adequate moisture, but can tolerate some drought. Optimal growth conditions include partial shade and temperatures between 15-25°C, although it can also grow in full sun and tolerate temperatures up to 30°C. To cultivate Angelica sinensis successfully, it is essential to plant the rhizomes in early spring or autumn, and to maintain a consistent moisture level, as it is susceptible to overwatering. Regular harvesting of the foliage can also help promote healthy growth and prevent the plant from flowering prematurely.
history: Angelica sinensis is a medicinal plant that originated in China, where it has been used for over 2,000 years to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. Its use was documented in the Shennong Bencaojing, a classic Chinese medical text, where it was described as having a "calming heat" effect. Traditionally, the plant was used to treat menstrual disorders, reduce inflammation, and improve circulation, with its root considered the most medicinal part. In traditional Chinese medicine, Angelica sinensis is often combined with other herbs to enhance its therapeutic effects, and it is still commonly used today in the form of teas, tinctures, and capsules. Modern studies have confirmed the plant's anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, supporting its traditional uses in treating conditions such as arthritis and menopausal symptoms.
